title: rubus hirtus var. purpureus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. purpureus, commonly known as the black raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of black raspberry include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and protect against certain types of cancer. Black raspberry is typically prepared as a tea, juice, or supplement, and is also used in jams, preserves, and other herbal preparations.
